Math4121Exam reviewsMath4121 Exam 1 Review

Math4121 Exam 1 Review

Range: Chapter 5 and 6 of Rudin. We skipped (and so you will not be tested on)

  • Differentiation of Vector Valued Functions (pp. 111-113)
  • Integration of Vector-Valued Function and Rectifiable Curves (pp.135-137)

You will also not be tested on Uniform Convergence and Integration, which we cover in class on Monday 2/10.

Chapter 5: Differentiation

Definition of the Derivative

Let fff be a real function defined on an closed interval [a,b][a,b][a,b]. We say that fff is differentiable at a point x∈[a,b]x \in [a,b]x∈[a,b] if the following limit exists:

f′(x)=lim⁡t→xf(t)−f(x)t−xf'(x) = \lim_{t\to x} \frac{f(t) - f(x)}{t - x}f′(x)=t→xlim​t−xf(t)−f(x)​

If the limit exists, we call it the derivative of fff at xxx and denote it by f′(x)f'(x)f′(x).

Theorem 5.2

Every differentiable function is continuous .

The converse is not true, consider f(x)=∣x∣f(x) = |x|f(x)=∣x∣.

Theorem 5.3

If f,gf,gf,g are differentiable at xxx, then

  1. (f+g)′(x)=f′(x)+g′(x)(f+g)'(x) = f'(x) + g'(x)(f+g)′(x)=f′(x)+g′(x)
  2. (fg)′(x)=f′(x)g(x)+f(x)g′(x)(fg)'(x) = f'(x)g(x) + f(x)g'(x)(fg)′(x)=f′(x)g(x)+f(x)g′(x)
  3. If g(x)≠0g(x) \neq 0g(x)=0, then (f/g)′(x)=(f′(x)g(x)−f(x)g′(x))/g(x)2(f/g)'(x) = (f'(x)g(x) - f(x)g'(x))/g(x)^2(f/g)′(x)=(f′(x)g(x)−f(x)g′(x))/g(x)2

Theorem 5.4

Constant function is differentiable and its derivative is 000.

Theorem 5.5

Chain rule: If fff is differentiable at xxx and ggg is differentiable at f(x)f(x)f(x), then the composite function g∘fg\circ fg∘f is differentiable at xxx and

(g∘f)′(x)=g′(f(x))f′(x)(g\circ f)'(x) = g'(f(x))f'(x)(g∘f)′(x)=g′(f(x))f′(x)

Theorem 5.8

The derivative of local extremum (∃δ>0\exists \delta > 0∃δ>0 s.t. f(x)≥f(y)f(x)\geq f(y)f(x)≥f(y) or f(x)≤f(y)f(x)\leq f(y)f(x)≤f(y) for all y∈(x−δ,x+δ)y\in (x-\delta,x+\delta)y∈(x−δ,x+δ)) is 000.

Theorem 5.9

Generalized mean value theorem: If f,gf,gf,g are differentiable on (a,b)(a,b)(a,b), then there exists a point x∈(a,b)x\in (a,b)x∈(a,b) such that

(f(b)−f(a))g′(x)=(g(b)−g(a))f′(x)(f(b)-f(a))g'(x) = (g(b)-g(a))f'(x)(f(b)−f(a))g′(x)=(g(b)−g(a))f′(x)

If we put g(x)=xg(x) = xg(x)=x, we get the mean value theorem.

f(b)−f(a)=f′(x)(b−a)f(b)-f(a) = f'(x)(b-a)f(b)−f(a)=f′(x)(b−a)

for some x∈(a,b)x\in (a,b)x∈(a,b).

Theorem 5.12

Intermediate value theorem:

If fff is differentiable on [a,b][a,b][a,b], for all λ\lambdaλ between f′(a)f'(a)f′(a) and f′(b)f'(b)f′(b), there exists a c∈(a,b)c\in (a,b)c∈(a,b) such that f′(x)=λf'(x) = \lambdaf′(x)=λ.

Theorem 5.13

L’Hôpital’s rule: If f,gf,gf,g are differentiable in (a,b)(a,b)(a,b) and g′(x)≠0g'(x) \neq 0g′(x)=0 for all x∈(a,b)x\in (a,b)x∈(a,b), where −∞≤a<b≤∞-\infty \leq a < b \leq \infty−∞≤a<b≤∞,

Suppose

f′(x)g′(x)→A as x→a\frac{f'(x)}{g'(x)} \to A \text{ as } x\to ag′(x)f′(x)​→A as x→a

If

f(x)→0,g(x)→0 as x→af(x) \to 0, g(x) \to 0 \text{ as } x\to af(x)→0,g(x)→0 as x→a

or if

g(x)→∞ as x→ag(x) \to \infty \text{ as } x\to ag(x)→∞ as x→a

then

lim⁡x→af(x)g(x)=A\lim_{x\to a} \frac{f(x)}{g(x)} = Ax→alim​g(x)f(x)​=A

Theorem 5.15

Taylor’s theorem: If fff is nnn times differentiable on [a,b][a,b][a,b], f(n−1)f^{(n-1)}f(n−1) is continuous on [a,b][a,b][a,b], and f(n)f^{(n)}f(n) exists on (a,b)(a,b)(a,b), for any distinct points α,β∈[a,b]\alpha, \beta \in [a,b]α,β∈[a,b], there exists a point x∈(α,β)x\in (\alpha, \beta)x∈(α,β) such that

f(β)=(∑k=0n−1f(k)(α)k!(β−α)k)+f(n)(x)n!(β−α)nf(\beta) =\left(\sum_{k=0}^{n-1} \frac{f^{(k)}(\alpha)}{k!}(\beta-\alpha)^k\right) + \frac{f^{(n)}(x)}{n!}(\beta-\alpha)^nf(β)=(k=0∑n−1​k!f(k)(α)​(β−α)k)+n!f(n)(x)​(β−α)n

Chapter 6: Riemann-Stieltjes Integration

Definition of the Integral

Let α\alphaα be a monotonically increasing function on [a,b][a,b][a,b].

A partition of [a,b][a,b][a,b] is a set of points P={x0,x1,⋯ ,xn}P = \{x_0, x_1, \cdots, x_n\}P={x0​,x1​,⋯,xn​} such that

a=x0<x1<⋯<xn=ba = x_0 < x_1 < \cdots < x_n = ba=x0​<x1​<⋯<xn​=b

Let Δαi=α(xi)−α(xi−1)\Delta \alpha_i = \alpha(x_{i}) - \alpha(x_{i-1})Δαi​=α(xi​)−α(xi−1​) for i=1,⋯ ,ni = 1, \cdots, ni=1,⋯,n.

Let mi=inf⁡{f(x):xi−1≤x≤xi}m_i = \inf \{f(x) : x_{i-1} \leq x \leq x_{i}\}mi​=inf{f(x):xi−1​≤x≤xi​} and Mi=sup⁡{f(x):xi−1≤x≤xi}M_i = \sup \{f(x) : x_{i-1} \leq x \leq x_{i}\}Mi​=sup{f(x):xi−1​≤x≤xi​} for i=1,⋯ ,ni = 1, \cdots, ni=1,⋯,n.

The lower sum of fff with respect to α\alphaα is

L(f,P,α)=∑i=1nmiΔαiL(f,P,\alpha) = \sum_{i=1}^{n} m_i \Delta \alpha_iL(f,P,α)=∑i=1n​mi​Δαi​

The upper sum of fff with respect to α\alphaα is

U(f,P,α)=∑i=1nMiΔαiU(f,P,\alpha) = \sum_{i=1}^{n} M_i \Delta \alpha_iU(f,P,α)=∑i=1n​Mi​Δαi​

Let ∫ab‾f(x)dα(x)=sup⁡PL(f,P,α)\overline{\int_a^b} f(x) d\alpha(x)=\sup_P L(f,P,\alpha)∫ab​​f(x)dα(x)=supP​L(f,P,α) and ∫ab‾f(x)dα(x)=inf⁡PU(f,P,α)\underline{\int_a^b} f(x) d\alpha(x)=\inf_P U(f,P,\alpha)∫ab​​f(x)dα(x)=infP​U(f,P,α).

If ∫ab‾f(x)dα(x)=∫ab‾f(x)dα(x)\overline{\int_a^b} f(x) d\alpha(x) = \underline{\int_a^b} f(x) d\alpha(x)∫ab​​f(x)dα(x)=∫ab​​f(x)dα(x), we say that fff is Riemann-Stieltjes integrable with respect to α\alphaα on [a,b][a,b][a,b] and we write

∫abf(x)dα(x)=∫ab‾f(x)dα(x)=∫ab‾f(x)dα(x)\int_a^b f(x) d\alpha(x) = \overline{\int_a^b} f(x) d\alpha(x) = \underline{\int_a^b} f(x) d\alpha(x)∫ab​f(x)dα(x)=∫ab​​f(x)dα(x)=∫ab​​f(x)dα(x)

Theorem 6.4

Refinement of partition will never make the lower sum smaller or the upper sum larger.

L(f,P,α)≤L(f,P∗,α)≤U(f,P∗,α)≤U(f,P,α)L(f,P,\alpha) \leq L(f,P^*,\alpha) \leq U(f,P^*,\alpha) \leq U(f,P,\alpha)L(f,P,α)≤L(f,P∗,α)≤U(f,P∗,α)≤U(f,P,α)

Theorem 6.5

∫ab‾f(x)dα(x)≤∫ab‾f(x)dα(x)\underline{\int_a^b} f(x) d\alpha(x) \leq \overline{\int_a^b} f(x) d\alpha(x)∫ab​​f(x)dα(x)≤∫ab​​f(x)dα(x)

Theorem 6.6

f∈R(α)f\in \mathscr{R}(\alpha)f∈R(α) on [a,b][a,b][a,b] if and only if for every ϵ>0\epsilon > 0ϵ>0, there exists a partition PPP of [a,b][a,b][a,b] such that

U(f,P,α)−L(f,P,α)<ϵU(f,P,\alpha) - L(f,P,\alpha) < \epsilonU(f,P,α)−L(f,P,α)<ϵ

Theorem 6.8

Every continuous function on a closed interval is Riemann-Stieltjes integrable with respect to any monotonically increasing function.

Theorem 6.9

If fff is monotonically increasing on [a,b][a,b][a,b] and α\alphaα is continuous on [a,b][a,b][a,b], then f∈R(α)f\in \mathscr{R}(\alpha)f∈R(α) on [a,b][a,b][a,b].

Key: We can repartition the interval [a,b][a,b][a,b] using fff.

Theorem 6.10

If fff is bounded on [a,b][a,b][a,b] and has only finitely many discontinuities on [a,b][a,b][a,b], then f∈R(α)f\in \mathscr{R}(\alpha)f∈R(α) on [a,b][a,b][a,b].

Key: We can use the bound and partition around the points of discontinuity to make the error arbitrary small.

Theorem 6.11

If f∈R(α)f\in \mathscr{R}(\alpha)f∈R(α) on [a,b][a,b][a,b], and m≤f(x)≤Mm\leq f(x) \leq Mm≤f(x)≤M for all x∈[a,b]x\in [a,b]x∈[a,b], and ϕ\phiϕ is a continuous function on [m,M][m,M][m,M], then ϕ∘f∈R(α)\phi\circ f\in \mathscr{R}(\alpha)ϕ∘f∈R(α) on [a,b][a,b][a,b].

Composition of bounded integrable functions and continuous functions is integrable.

Theorem 6.12

Properties of the integral:

Let f,g∈R(α)f,g\in \mathscr{R}(\alpha)f,g∈R(α) on [a,b][a,b][a,b], and ccc be a constant. Then

  1. f+g∈R(α)f+g\in \mathscr{R}(\alpha)f+g∈R(α) on [a,b][a,b][a,b] and ∫ab(f(x)+g(x))dα(x)=∫abf(x)dα(x)+∫abg(x)dα(x)\int_a^b (f(x) + g(x)) d\alpha(x) = \int_a^b f(x) d\alpha(x) + \int_a^b g(x) d\alpha(x)∫ab​(f(x)+g(x))dα(x)=∫ab​f(x)dα(x)+∫ab​g(x)dα(x)
  2. cf∈R(α)cf\in \mathscr{R}(\alpha)cf∈R(α) on [a,b][a,b][a,b] and ∫abcf(x)dα(x)=c∫abf(x)dα(x)\int_a^b cf(x) d\alpha(x) = c\int_a^b f(x) d\alpha(x)∫ab​cf(x)dα(x)=c∫ab​f(x)dα(x)
  3. f∈R(α)f\in \mathscr{R}(\alpha)f∈R(α) on [a,b][a,b][a,b] and c∈[a,b]c\in [a,b]c∈[a,b], then ∫abf(x)dα(x)=∫acf(x)dα(x)+∫cbf(x)dα(x)\int_a^b f(x) d\alpha(x) = \int_a^c f(x) d\alpha(x) + \int_c^b f(x) d\alpha(x)∫ab​f(x)dα(x)=∫ac​f(x)dα(x)+∫cb​f(x)dα(x).
  4. Favorite Estimate: If ∣f(x)∣≤M|f(x)| \leq M∣f(x)∣≤M for all x∈[a,b]x\in [a,b]x∈[a,b], then ∣∫abf(x)dα(x)∣≤M(α(b)−α(a))\left|\int_a^b f(x) d\alpha(x)\right| \leq M(\alpha(b)-\alpha(a))​∫ab​f(x)dα(x)​≤M(α(b)−α(a)).
  5. If f∈R(β)f\in \mathscr{R}(\beta)f∈R(β) on [a,b][a,b][a,b], then ∫abf(x)d(α+β)=∫abf(x)dα+∫abf(x)dβ\int_a^b f(x) d(\alpha+\beta) = \int_a^b f(x) d\alpha + \int_a^b f(x) d\beta∫ab​f(x)d(α+β)=∫ab​f(x)dα+∫ab​f(x)dβ.

Theorem 6.13

If f,g∈R(α)f,g\in \mathscr{R}(\alpha)f,g∈R(α) on [a,b][a,b][a,b], then

  1. fg∈R(α)fg\in \mathscr{R}(\alpha)fg∈R(α) on [a,b][a,b][a,b]
  2. ∣f∣∈R(α)|f|\in \mathscr{R}(\alpha)∣f∣∈R(α) on [a,b][a,b][a,b] and ∣∫abf(x)dα(x)∣≤∫ab∣f(x)∣dα(x)\left|\int_a^b f(x) d\alpha(x)\right| \leq \int_a^b |f(x)| d\alpha(x)​∫ab​f(x)dα(x)​≤∫ab​∣f(x)∣dα(x)

Key: (1), use Theorem 6.12, 6.11 to build up fgfgfg from (f+g)2−f2−g2(f+g)^2-f^2-g^2(f+g)2−f2−g2. (2), take ϕ(x)=∣x∣\phi(x) = |x|ϕ(x)=∣x∣ in Theorem 6.11.

Theorem 6.14

Integration over indicator functions:

If a<s<ba<s<ba<s<b, fff is bounded on [a,b][a,b][a,b], and fff is continuous at sss, and α(x)=I(x−s)\alpha(x)=I(x-s)α(x)=I(x−s), then

∫abf(x)dα(x)=f(s)\int_a^b f(x) d\alpha(x) = f(s)∫ab​f(x)dα(x)=f(s)

Key: Note the max difference can be made only occurs at sss.

Theorem 6.15

Integration over step functions:

If α(x)=∑i=1nciI(x−xi)\alpha(x) = \sum_{i=1}^{n} c_i I(x-x_i)α(x)=∑i=1n​ci​I(x−xi​) for x∈[a,b]x\in [a,b]x∈[a,b], then

∫abf(x)dα(x)=∑i=1ncif(xi)\int_a^b f(x) d\alpha(x) = \sum_{i=1}^{n} c_i f(x_i)∫ab​f(x)dα(x)=i=1∑n​ci​f(xi​)

Theorem 6.21

Fundamental theorem of calculus:

Let f∈R(α)f\in \mathscr{R}(\alpha)f∈R(α) on [a,b][a,b][a,b], and F(x)=∫axf(t)dα(t)F(x) = \int_a^x f(t) d\alpha(t)F(x)=∫ax​f(t)dα(t). Then

  1. FFF is continuous on [a,b][a,b][a,b]
  2. If fff is continuous at x∈[a,b]x\in [a,b]x∈[a,b], then FFF is differentiable at xxx and F′(x)=f(x)F'(x) = f(x)F′(x)=f(x)

Chapter 7: Sequence and Series of Functions

Example of non-Riemann integrable function

lim⁡m→∞lim⁡n→∞(cos⁡(m!πx))2n={1x∈Q0x∉Q\lim_{m\to \infty} \lim_{n\to \infty} (\cos(m!\pi x))^{2n}=\begin{cases} 1 & x\in \mathbb{Q} \\ 0 & x\notin \mathbb{Q} \end{cases}limm→∞​limn→∞​(cos(m!πx))2n={10​x∈Qx∈/Q​

This function is everywhere discontinuous and not Riemann integrable.

Uniform Convergence

Definition 7.7

A sequence of functions {fn}\{f_n\}{fn​} converges uniformly to fff on EEE if for every ϵ>0\epsilon > 0ϵ>0, there exists a positive integer NNN such that

∣fn(x)−f(x)∣<ϵ for all x∈E and n≥N|f_n(x) - f(x)| < \epsilon \text{ for all } x\in E \text{ and } n\geq N∣fn​(x)−f(x)∣<ϵ for all x∈E and n≥N

If EEE is a point, then that’s the common definition of convergence.

If we have uniform convergence, then we can swap the order of limits.

Theorem 7.16

If {fn}∈R(α)\{f_n\}\in \mathscr{R}(\alpha){fn​}∈R(α) on [a,b][a,b][a,b], and {fn}\{f_n\}{fn​} converges uniformly to fff on [a,b][a,b][a,b], then

∫abf(x)dα(x)=lim⁡n→∞∫abfn(x)dα(x)\int_a^b f(x) d\alpha(x) = \lim_{n\to \infty} \int_a^b f_n(x) d\alpha(x)∫ab​f(x)dα(x)=n→∞lim​∫ab​fn​(x)dα(x)

Key: Use the definition of uniform convergence to bound the difference between the integral of the limit and the limit of the integral. ∫ab(f−fn)dα≤∣f−fn∣∫abdα=∣f−fn∣(α(b)−α(a))\int_a^b (f-f_n)d\alpha \leq |f-f_n| \int_a^b d\alpha = |f-f_n| (\alpha(b)-\alpha(a))∫ab​(f−fn​)dα≤∣f−fn​∣∫ab​dα=∣f−fn​∣(α(b)−α(a)).
